A local branch Of the state tuber-
culosis league is about to be organ
lied in Spokane. Miss Bethesda
l.. Beals, executive secretary, with
headquarters at Seattle, has ar


rived in this city, and meeting


will be held in the chamber of
commerce, in the Hutton block,
Monday evening at 8 o'clock, for


the purpose Of organizing said
branch.


Jack SpauIding, who has lived in
| Spokane for 20 years, will be mar


ried today to Miss NJ. c. Mortimer,


a private nurse, in Justice Suckers
court.


Supe L. r. Horton OF
the Anti-Saloon league has sent out
5000 cards to county voters stating
that he expects the county to go
dry at the next election.
The father Of Harlow Rice, II


years old, attempted to take hand


in the lads trial at the juvenile


court before Judge Hinkle yester-
day, and was arrested. But after


a lecture by the court was permit
ted to 60 without a fine.
A concert and dance Is to be


given by the Spokane Swedish
| Male chorus next Wednesday at


Bouley hall, $40 1-2 Riverside.
The report that has been circu-


lated about the courthouse that


Judge Henry L. KEENAN, of the SUI


perior court is a candidate for the


supreme bench has been denied by
Judge KEENAN, himself.
Police last night were called upon


to dispense a gathering of |. w. WIS


that had surrounded James Ken
| nedy, an alleged "scab," who had


taken the place of one of the strik-
ing dishwashers at the Jim and
John restaurant, on Front avenue.


The men were trying to dissuade


Kennedy from further continuing


with the job, when the police ap-


preached and dispersed the crowd.


The sympathies of the /. w. w.


are with the striking cooks and
| walters.


The jury in the superior court
before Judge E. H. Sullivan, after


short deliberation, brought in a


verdict for $1,000 in favor of Eliza


beth Hart, who Is suing the city of
Spokane for injuries received by
falling into an open ditch.
The funeral of Ruth Mary Pull


man, infant child of Mr.. and Mrs.


j. II. Pullman. S521 Grant street,
| will be held from the family resi-
dence tomorrow at 2:30 o'clock.
